tactical.bear_case: RSI 62 after a sharp multi-week rally into freight rate weakness — spot rates (Drewry WCI) have been rolling over from 2024 Red Sea highs, and a -4% candle can mark the start of mean reversion back toward the SMA50 at $12.16, a ~11% downside gap.
tactical.bull_case: The -4% is a single liquidity-driven ADR move; underlying Copenhagen shares plus a still-golden-cross structure and price holding above both MAs suggests dip-buyers defend $13, with any Suez re-escalation spiking rates and the tape instantly.
tactical.falsifier: Drewry WCI composite turning back UP >10% week-over-week (Suez/Red Sea re-disruption) would flip this to a buy-lean as rates re-accelerate.

positional.bear_case: Massive container newbuild deliveries in 2025-26 expand effective capacity just as Red Sea reroute demand fades, pressuring spot rates and earnings — the golden cross becomes a lagging artifact of a peak already passed.
positional.bull_case: Maersk's integrator logistics strategy and disciplined Gemini network with Hapag-Lloyd could sustain above-trend earnings; sustained price above SMA50/200 reflects a market re-rating the franchise, not just spot rates.
positional.falsifier: A decisive close below the SMA50 ($12.16) breaking the golden-cross structure would flip the positional lean to buy-side (oversold value) rather than trim.

strategic.bear_case: If 2025-26 is actually a cyclical TOP disguised by Red Sea-inflated rates, then 25x P/E is masking peak-ish earnings and reverts toward trough; record orderbook deliveries crush rates into 2026, and 0.71x P/B can fall further toward 0.4-0.5x as it did in prior down-cycles (2016, 2019).
strategic.bull_case: Below-replacement P/B with logistics priced at zero is a multi-year value setup; capital returns (buybacks/dividends), Gemini network cost savings, and any structural Suez avoidance keeping rates elevated drive a re-rating toward book and beyond.
strategic.falsifier: P/B re-rating above ~1.0x (signaling earnings/cycle peak being priced) OR a sustained freight rate collapse with WCI falling below pre-2024 baseline would flip the strategic sign to trim.

tactical.bear_case: 23% above SMA50 with RSI near 68 after a vertical move; any disappointment in semicap commentary or a China export headline could trigger a fast 5-8% pullback from these stretched levels.
tactical.bull_case: EUV/High-NA demand momentum is real and order flow could keep pushing the stock; momentum names like ASML routinely run RSI 70+ for weeks without reverting, so fading strength here loses.
tactical.falsifier: A clean break and hold above recent highs on accelerating volume (or a strong upside order/booking pre-announcement) would flip this to a buy-side tactical read.

positional.bear_case: The price-to-SMA50 gap of 23% is historically wide; reversion to the 50-day alone implies ~18% downside, and consolidations after such runs are common.
positional.bull_case: Backlog visibility of 2-3 years and High-NA ramp give fundamental support; the trend has been a reliable up-and-to-the-right channel and dips have been shallow and quickly bought.
positional.falsifier: A pullback of >10% toward the SMA50 that holds, or a backlog/book-to-bill acceleration, would shift positional to neutral-to-buy.

strategic.bear_case: If 2026 EPS recovery disappoints (China export controls tighten further, memory/logic capex digestion lingers), the elevated multiple compresses and the stock could derate 15-20% even with intact long-term story.
strategic.bull_case: Sole EUV supplier with widening High-NA lead ($350M+/tool), AI-driven leading-edge demand, and durable double-digit growth; at this scarcity premium the multiple can stay elevated and compound, making any neutral-to-trim score wrong.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E falling below ~25x (via EPS beats or a >15% drawdown) would flip strategic decisively to the buy side; conversely forward P/E pushing above 45x on flat estimates would justify a stronger trim.

tactical.bear_case: ENB has fallen two consecutive sessions (56.85→55.93) and a break of the 54.8 SMA50 could accelerate selling if the 10Y keeps creeping toward 4.5%+, making the thin 46bps yield spread look even worse.
tactical.bull_case: A modest pullback near the rising SMA50 is a classic support bounce setup for an income name; any soft inflation print or dovish Fed/BoC comment could spark a sympathetic rate-driven rally given ENB's bond proxy behavior.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive RSI move below 35 (oversold on a rate spike) would flip this to a buy; an RSI surge above 70 on no fundamental change would flip it more positive.

positional.bear_case: 46bps spread is near the bottom of ENB's historical range — when the 10Y is paying you almost as much as the pipeline equity with full downside risk, the relative-value case for trimming is strong, especially with 14 straight TRIM days validated by yesterday's drop.
positional.bull_case: The thin spread is partly a function of the 10Y being elevated, not ENB being expensive in absolute terms; if BoC/Fed begin cutting, the 10Y falls and ENB re-rates higher fast. LNG Canada Phase 1 first cargoes and Phase 2 sanctioning are near-term volume catalysts that the spread metric ignores entirely.
positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above ~150bps — whether via an ENB price drop or a 10Y decline to ~3.4% — would flip this to a buy. A dividend growth guidance raise on LNG/gas volume strength would also turn the sign negative.

strategic.bear_case: With real yields at 2.16% and the curve only +40bps, a bond-proxy paying 4.9% at 25x earnings offers a thin long-run risk premium; capital is better deployed elsewhere in the book until the spread normalizes, and pipeline permitting/regulatory risk caps multiple expansion.
strategic.bull_case: ENB's LNG Canada and Gulf Coast gas infrastructure positions it for a structural multi-year volume tailwind as global LNG demand grows; the franchise is effectively un-replicable and the dividend has compounded for ~29 years. A rate-cutting cycle would re-rate the entire midstream-as-income complex.
strategic.falsifier: Start of a sustained BoC/Fed easing cycle (10Y trending below 3.5%) or LNG Canada Phase 2 sanctioning with raised dividend-growth guidance would flip this negative; a dividend coverage deterioration or major pipeline obsolescence event would push it more positive.

tactical.bear_case: GLNCY failing to follow a +4.48% COPX day signals the market is discounting the copper beta — coal ESG overhang and weak zinc/cobalt cap upside, so the ADR stalls near $15.78 resistance and rolls over while pure-copper names run.
tactical.bull_case: GLNCY/COPX spread of -4.16pp is exactly the catch-up setup; with price holding above a rising 50d and RSI mid-range (room to run), a single confirming session closes the gap and pushes GLNCY toward $16.50.
tactical.falsifier: GLNCY closing below the 50d SMA ($15.34) on rising volume would flip this from neutral-trim toward a tactical buy-the-dip or confirm breakdown depending on copper; a decisive break of $16.20 with volume flips to clear buy.

positional.bear_case: COPX outperformance may be persistent rather than mean-reverting — if it reflects pure-copper repricing (Freeport/Southern) that GLNCY structurally can't match due to thermal coal dilution and trading-arm opacity, the spread stays wide and GLNCY underperforms peers for weeks.
positional.bull_case: Copper at multi-month highs with tight LME inventories plus tight HY spreads = classic mid-cycle industrial demand; GLNCY's 30% copper EBITDA plus mispriced trading arm means the catch-up to COPX could be a 5-8% relative move over coming weeks.
positional.falsifier: A Chinese property/PMI disappointment or LME copper breaking back below its recent range would invalidate the catch-up thesis and flip the position score positive.

strategic.bear_case: Coal is ~25% of EBITDA and faces structural ESG de-rating and price normalization off post-2022 highs; combined with a P/B at 1.64 (not below book), the cheap-cyclical setup is less compelling than a true sub-0.8x P/B trough, and a global manufacturing slowdown would deepen the trough rather than recover it.
strategic.bull_case: Trough earnings (248x P/E) plus a structural copper supply deficit and an under-valued trading arm mean normalized EPS could be multiples of current — buying near the trough with secular copper tailwinds and a 2.15% yield is the textbook cyclical entry.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the trough is deepening — a sustained copper rollover below cycle support plus Chinese demand contraction and widening HY OAS — would flip the strategic score positive toward trim.

tactical.bear_case: The +5.13% pop is a bounce within a firmly intact downtrend (below both MAs); momentum assets routinely give back single-day rallies, and RSI 43 has plenty of room to roll back over toward 30.
tactical.bull_case: Sharp rallies from oversold conditions in BTC tend to extend over 5 days; my tactical BUY hit rate is 100% on small sample and today's move could be the start of a multi-day mean-reversion squeeze.
tactical.falsifier: A reversal that erases today's +5% gain and closes red below $36 within 1-2 sessions would flip the tactical read to neutral/sell.

positional.bear_case: Trend intact below both MAs; historical 6.7% hit rate warns the buy reflex has failed repeatedly; 52w low break risks cascade.
positional.bull_case: Deep-discount oversold with uncapped BTC upside and structural ETF bid; +5% rally hints at turn.
positional.falsifier: Decisive close below $33.48 52w low on rising outflows.

strategic.bear_case: If this cycle is genuinely breaking the 4-year pattern downward due to institutionalization dampening volatility/returns, the long-run store-of-value compounding thesis weakens and there is no strategic edge to owning at any phase.
strategic.bull_case: Each cycle has expanded BTC's holder base and legitimacy; sovereign/corporate treasury adoption and ETF rails create a structurally higher floor, making deep-drawdown accumulation strategically sound for multi-year holders.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence of sustained, multi-quarter ETF net outflows alongside declining on-chain active addresses would signal structural demand erosion and flip the strategic sign positive.

tactical.bear_case: Two consecutive down days (-2.63% then -1.74%) hint at short-term distribution; if a broader EM risk-off develops, low-liquidity ADRs like KOF can gap down regardless of fundamentals.
tactical.bull_case: The pullback to neutral RSI from a golden-cross posture offers a clean dip-buy entry; a stable peso and calm volatility regime mean any oversold bounce off the 50-day could resume the uptrend quickly.
tactical.falsifier: RSI pushing below ~35 on accelerating peso weakness, or above ~70 on a peso-driven surge, would move the tactical score off zero.

positional.bear_case: Back-to-back declines totaling ~4% threaten the 50-day SMA at $104.65; a break would void the golden-cross posture and invite trend-follower exits in a thin name.
positional.bull_case: Price holds well above both SMAs with the 200-day rising at $96.67; this defensive compounder typically sees 50-day pullbacks bought, especially with a stable peso.
positional.falsifier: A sustained close below the $104.65 50-day SMA with the 50/200 spread compressing flips the posture to neutral/negative.

strategic.bear_case: Structural peso weakness from a dovish Banxico, US tariff threats on Mexico, and fiscal slippage under the Sheinbaum administration could durably impair USD-denominated returns; staples multiples also de-rate if real rates (TIPS 2.16%) stay elevated, pressuring the dividend's relative appeal.
strategic.bull_case: Nearshoring structurally lifts Mexican formal employment and consumption, KOF compounds dividends and reinvests in digital/B2B (Juntos+) ahead of Arca Continental, and a peso that holds or strengthens delivers a tailwind to both earnings translation and the ADR — a quality EM compounder at an undemanding multiple.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained MXN/USD break beyond ~19 (sharp peso depreciation) or a structural margin breakdown below historical bottler norms would flip the strategic sign to positive (trim).

tactical.bear_case: Below both SMA50 ($320.92) and SMA200 ($315.28) with no base formed; -19.8% drawdown can extend toward the $243 low if the op-margin compression (9.61%) is read as structural rather than transient. Knife still falling — yesterday's bounce thesis failed.
tactical.bull_case: A -19.8% drawdown in the smallest, highest-quality-trajectory prime during an active great-power-competition regime is a gift; RSI 44 leaves room to rally, and any program win or B/B beat snaps it back fast given low float volatility.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive break and hold below the $290 area (toward 52w-low retest) on accelerating volume with no catalyst would flip this to a sell/avoid; conversely a reclaim of SMA50 confirms the buy.

positional.bear_case: 9.61% op margin signals real cost/integration (Aerojet) or program-mix pressure; a drawdown to 44.5% of range with deteriorating margins is value-trap territory, and EPS revisions may be drifting negative, removing the positional tailwind entirely.
positional.bull_case: Backlog compounder with multi-year DoD visibility, trading at a real discount on forward earnings; below both MAs after a 20% pullment in a structurally bid defense sector (ITA tailwind) is exactly the entry the archetype rewards over 20d.
positional.falsifier: Latest earnings call showing book-to-bill <0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or 90d EPS revisions turning down >1%, would confirm thesis erosion and flip this positive.

strategic.bear_case: Op margin at 9.61% with supply-chain (titanium/energetics) constraints could cap the re-rating thesis; if DoD budget slips into CR/sequester uncertainty in FY27 negotiations, the defense bid fades and the cohort premium (even if trailing-distorted) reverts.
strategic.bull_case: Forward P/E discount intact, great-power-competition regime durable, Golden Dome / munitions / Aerojet propellant demand multi-year, and cohort compression toward larger primes offers structural re-rating for the smallest Big-5 name.
strategic.falsifier: A confirmed shift in DoD budget phase to sequester/CR uncertainty (failed NDAA, budget caps reinstated) or geopolitical de-escalation toward a peace dividend would flip this firmly positive (trim).

tactical.bear_case: LIN pinned at all-time highs (99.2%) with RSI 62 leaves zero cushion; any broad QUAL-factor rotation or a single soft semi-capex headline could trigger mean-reversion from a stretched technical perch, and DXY at 120 is a live FX headwind into the next print.
tactical.bull_case: Above both MAs in a confirmed golden-cross uptrend with VIX subdued; quality compounders at highs in a neutral regime tend to keep grinding higher as defensive bid persists, and there is no momentum-breaking news today.
tactical.falsifier: A break below the 50-day SMA ($504.93) on elevated volume, or a sharp VIX spike >25 with LIN underperforming SPY, would flip this to trim-side positive.

positional.bear_case: Position at 99.2% of 52w range means the positional setup is fully priced; a peer catch-up (APD reset under new CEO closing the valuation gap) would compress LIN's premium and remove the relative-value support, turning the spread tailwind into a headwind.
positional.bull_case: AI.PA leading by 1.13pp means LIN is relatively cheap vs its own historical premium; tight and tightening BBB spreads de-risk LIN's large on-site project backlog economics, supporting continued backlog conversion and a structural bid under the stock.
positional.falsifier: LIN/peer spreads widening +2pp in LIN's favor (premium re-extending) plus a break below the 50-day SMA would flip this positive; conversely sustained spread compression confirms the buy.

strategic.bear_case: If the margin compression is real rather than FX-driven — a true mix/cost squeeze as hydrogen project ramp-up dilutes near-term returns — then the premium multiple (34x P/E, 1.22% yield near cycle-low) is vulnerable to de-rating, especially with TIPS real yield at 2.16% raising the discount rate on a long-duration compounder.
strategic.bull_case: A >$1B blue/green H2 mega-project win (LIN is global #1 H2 producer), IRA 45V guidance broadening green economics, or a semi-fab on-site contract wave (TSMC AZ, Samsung TX, Intel OH, Micron NY) would re-accelerate backlog and validate the premium, making today's valuation look reasonable for the structural growth runway.
strategic.falsifier: A confirmed mega-scale H2 offtake/semi-fab contract announcement or an earnings beat with backlog acceleration would flip this strategic score negative (buy); conversely two consecutive quarters of genuine ex-FX op-margin compression with negative price/mix would push it materially more positive.

tactical.bear_case: Price is below both 50d ($412) and 200d ($453) in a death-cross configuration — a -28% drawdown can extend, and a +2.24% bounce in a downtrend is often a dead-cat. RSI 42 is not deeply oversold, so there is room to fall further before a true washout. If the bounce fails at the 50d, momentum traders pile on the short side.
tactical.bull_case: A -28% drawdown in a 46.75% op-margin monopoly with the strongest AI distribution moat rarely persists. RSI rebounding from oversold plus a notable up-day on low realized vol is a textbook mean-reversion setup; mechanical QUAL buyers and buyback support provide a floor.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive break below the 52w low of $356.28 on rising volume, confirming the downtrend continues rather than reverses, would flip this to neutral/trim.

positional.bear_case: MA structure is below both averages and falling — major compounders that break their 200d can stay broken for quarters (see 2022). The 20d forward window for positional has a 42.9% hit rate, below coin-flip. If Azure CC decelerates below 22% at next print, the 'cheap' valuation re-rates lower as the secular growth premium compresses.
positional.bull_case: 21.7% range position on a 46.75%-margin monopoly with ~10% buyback yield baseline is a once-in-a-cycle entry. Trailing P/E 23.5x is a 14.5% discount to GOOGL/META/AAPL cohort — deserved-quality names rarely trade at a discount. Mechanical QUAL factor bid plus buyback floor compound the asymmetry.
positional.falsifier: Azure constant-currency growth printing below 22% with capex still rising 40%+ (ROI questions) would impair the compounder thesis and flip positional to neutral/trim.

strategic.bear_case: Real rates at 2.16% pressure long-duration growth multiples; if the Fed stays higher-for-longer, the strategic premium compresses. OpenAI partnership governance/AGI-clause risk and rising capex with uncertain ROI could cap returns. ~50% non-US revenue with DXY at 120 is an FX drag.
strategic.bull_case: Azure as the primary AI infra rail, OpenAI proprietary distribution, and Copilot monetization inflection give MSFT secular growth ASML-like durability at a cohort discount. If Azure CC re-accelerates above 30% and Copilot seats inflect, the strategic re-rate is large from a depressed base.
strategic.falsifier: A structural OpenAI partnership unwind/loss of exclusivity, or Azure CC sustained below 22% across two quarters, would break the strategic thesis and flip the sign.

tactical.bear_case: The -4.78% drop on a $95 WTI day is a tell that something Petrobras-specific is wrong — a dividend cut headline or Lula interference event — and oversold can stay oversold when there's a fundamental catalyst. Breaking the 200-day SMA opens a path to lower lows.
tactical.bull_case: RSI 31 at the 200-day SMA with WTI at $95 is a textbook mean-reversion buy; my one historical tactical buy hit 100%. A bounce back toward the SMA50 gap is plausible once the headline-driven selling exhausts.
tactical.falsifier: A confirmed close decisively below the 200-day SMA ($15.47) with accelerating volume, or a concrete adverse Petrobras headline (dividend cut/CEO change), would flip this positive (trim).

positional.bear_case: The -22.6% gap below SMA50 reflects a genuine deterioration in sentiment — likely political interference fears or fuel-pricing-policy concerns — and at $95 oil the stock SHOULD be ripping; the fact it isn't means the market is pricing idiosyncratic Brazilian risk that won't resolve on a 30-90 day horizon.
positional.bull_case: At $95 WTI a sub-$16 Petrobras with a 9.4% yield and pre-salt breakeven near $35 is generating massive free cash; once political fear subsides, mean reversion toward the $20 SMA50 offers 25%+ upside plus dividend.
positional.falsifier: Sustained WTI break below ~$70 or a formal dividend-policy downgrade/forced-capex mandate from the Lula government would flip this to trim.

strategic.bear_case: 5x trailing P/E at $95 oil signals peak earnings — when oil rolls over, earnings and the dividend both compress, and Lula's government has a track record of diverting cash flow into below-market fuel pricing and uneconomic refining capex, structurally impairing returns to minority holders.
strategic.bull_case: Petrobras at 5x earnings, 1.5x book, 9.4% yield with a $35 breakeven is one of the cheapest large-cap oil producers globally; if the dividend policy holds, total return (yield + buybacks + modest re-rating) is compelling over 12-24 months versus IOC peers trading richer.
strategic.falsifier: A government move to override the dividend formula / mandate large value-destructive capex, or WTI sustained below pre-salt-comfortable levels (~$60), would flip this to trim.
